approval_request.minimum_next_approval_scope: A human must approve the exact account/profile route, portfolio representation, target job or no-job decision, proposal text, deliverable/revision boundary, terms/fees/payment/tax assumptions, privacy/data handling, credential policy, anti-spam relevance, and public action before any external Upwork step.

next_local_action: If the operator approves this packet, create an exact-scope service request for one Upwork AI Integration Workflow Audit account/profile/proposal review. Do not log in, create/edit a profile or portfolio, choose a live job, send a proposal, message a client, accept terms, or touch payment/tax setup from this packet alone.
pre_send_checklist: Verify the offer claim is limited to a supervised AI integration workflow audit and does not promise production implementation.
Verify profile and portfolio wording contains no fake Upwork history, ratings, earnings, client names, or real-world outcomes.
Verify the synthetic proof asset is labeled synthetic and is not presented as a client case study.
Verify any target job is exact, relevant, low-risk, and manually chosen by the human operator.
Verify the proposal asks for non-sensitive workflow notes only and excludes credentials, account access, production systems, and private regulated data.
Verify Upwork terms, fees, tax/payment, identity, worker-classification, and anti-spam obligations are acceptable to the human operator.

selected_offer.decision_note: Best first move: high official demand signal, strongest local proof, and no need for production access.
deliverables: Map one described workflow from non-sensitive notes.
Identify AI integration points, approval gates, rollback paths, and credential boundaries.
Deliver a local blueprint plus synthetic proof asset.
selected_offer.skill_fit: AI Integration / Scripting & Automation / Marketing Automation
selected_offer.why_first: It best matches Upwork's official 2026 AI Integration growth signal while reusing existing local workflow-audit proof assets and avoiding account, proposal, payment, credential, model/API, and public-action gates.
